title: Takht Sri Patna Sahib
tagline: Takht Sri Patna Sahib — heritage city of Bihar

overview.short: Takht Sri Patna Sahib also known as Takhat Sri Harimandir Ji, Patna Sahib, is one of the five takhts of the Sikhs, located in Patna, Bihar, India.
overview.description: Takht Sri Patna Sahib also known as Takhat Sri Harimandir Ji, Patna Sahib, is one of the five takhts of the Sikhs, located in Patna, Bihar, India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Patan Devi
topPlaces.description: Patan Devi, also called Maa Patneshwari, is the oldest and one of the most sacred temples of Patna, Bihar. It is regarded as one of the 51 Siddha Shakta pithas in India.

topPlaces.name: Kamaldah Jain temple
topPlaces.description: The Kamaldah Jain temple, located in Patna, Bihar, is one of the oldest Jain temples belonging to the Śvētāmbara sect of Jainism.

topPlaces.name: Gulzarbagh
topPlaces.description: Gulzarbagh is a neighbourhood of Patna in the Bihar state of India. It is located at 25°36'0N 85°12'0E with an altitude of 45 metres (150 feet).

topPlaces.name: Gaighat, Patna
topPlaces.description: Gaighat is a neighbourhood of Patna in the eastern state of Bihar in India. It is located on the southern bank of the Ganges River in Patna.

topPlaces.name: Agam Kuan
topPlaces.description: Agam Kuan ("unfathomable well") is an ancient well and archaeological site in Patna, Bihar, India. It is said to date back to the period of Mauryan emperor, Ashoka (304–232 BCE).

topPlaces.name: Mahatma Gandhi Setu
topPlaces.description: Mahatma Gandhi Setu (also called Gandhi Setu or Ganga Setu) is a bridge over the river Ganga in Bihar, India, connecting Patna in the south to Hajipur in the north. Its length of 5,750 metres (18,860 ft) makes it the fourth-longest river bridge in India.

topPlaces.name: Baldwin Academy
topPlaces.description: Baldwin Academy is a senior secondary, co-educational, English medium school located in Patna, Bihar, India. It is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Didarganj Yakshi
topPlaces.description: The Didarganj Yakshi (or Didarganj Chauri Bearer; Hindi: दीदारगंज यक्षी) is one of the finest examples of very early Indian stone statues. It used to be dated to the 3rd century BCE, as it has the fine Mauryan polish associated with Mauryan art, but another Yakshi is also found but without polish so it is also dated to approximately the 2nd century CE, based on the similarity of analysis of shape and ornamentation, or the 1st century CE. The treatment of the forelock in particular is said to be characteristically Kushan.

items.note: Patan Devi, also called Maa Patneshwari, is the oldest and one of the most sacred temples of Patna, Bihar. It is regarded as one of the 51 Siddha Shakta pithas in India.
The Kamaldah Jain temple, located in Patna, Bihar, is one of the oldest Jain temples belonging to the Śvētāmbara sect of Jainism.
Gulzarbagh is a neighbourhood of Patna in the Bihar state of India. It is located at 25°36'0N 85°12'0E with an altitude of 45 metres (150 feet).
Gaighat is a neighbourhood of Patna in the eastern state of Bihar in India. It is located on the southern bank of the Ganges River in Patna.
Agam Kuan ("unfathomable well") is an ancient well and archaeological site in Patna, Bihar, India. It is said to date back to the period of Mauryan emperor, Ashoka (304–232 BCE).

Mahatma Gandhi Setu (also called Gandhi Setu or Ganga Setu) is a bridge over the river Ganga in Bihar, India, connecting Patna in the south to Hajipur in the north. Its length of 5,750 metres (18,860 ft) makes it the fourth-longest river bridge in India.
Baldwin Academy is a senior secondary, co-educational, English medium school located in Patna, Bihar, India. It is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.

The Didarganj Yakshi (or Didarganj Chauri Bearer; Hindi: दीदारगंज यक्षी) is one of the finest examples of very early Indian stone statues. It used to be dated to the 3rd century BCE, as it has the fine Mauryan polish associated with Mauryan art, but another Yakshi is also found but without polish so it is also dated to approximately the 2nd century CE, based on the similarity of analysis of shape and ornamentation, or the 1st century CE. The treatment of the forelock in particular is said to be characteristically Kushan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Takht Sri Patna Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Takht Sri Patna Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Takht Sri Patna Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Patna Junction (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Takht Sri Patna Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Takht Sri Patna Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Takht Sri Patna Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Takht Sri Patna Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Takht Sri Patna Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Patan Devi, Kamaldah Jain temple, Gulzarbagh, Gaighat, Patna, Agam Kuan.
